verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its primary localization is cytoplasmic/inner-leaflet. However, SRC has well-documented cancer-cell ecto-display: in tumor cells (colorectal, breast, others), active SRC is found on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — a phenomenon studied as 'ecto-kinase' or 'cancer ecto-Src'. This cancer-restricted outer-leaflet pool has been exploited therapeutically and detected by cell-surface biotinylation and antibody-accessibility assays on intact cells. This qualifies as cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation). Additionally, activation-induced surface translocation in immune/neural contexts has been described. The protein body does not reach the outer leaflet under baseline non-transformed conditions, but documented cancer ecto-pool justifies contextual rather than no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and mechanism of outer-leaflet display in cancer cells is debated; some reports attribute SRC surface detection to artefact or inner-leaflet proximity rather than true ecto-kinase activity.
